pi_items.source: Albedo literature: old/compressed snow typically ranges 0.55-0.65; verified via research on snow reflectance properties (https://www.researchgate.net/figure/Typical-albedo-values-for-fresh-snow-old-dry-snow-and-melting-snow-in-CLASS-model_tbl3_376010554)
Albedo literature: melting snow typically has albedo around 0.4; consistent with seasonal snow reflectance transitions (https://mynasadata.larc.nasa.gov/basic-page/albedo-values)
DOE-2 and eQUEST documentation confirm 0.2 as standard default for bare ground/grass reflectance (https://energy-models.com/forum/groundwater-reflectance)

pi_items.verified_value: Buffalo, NY averages 92 inches (2.3 m) annual snowfall with over 50% from lake-effect snow; snow typically covers ground from Christmas to early March
pi_items.source: NOAA/National Weather Service Buffalo Office climate data (https://www.weather.gov/buf/BUFsnowdepth) and (https://www.currentresults.com/Weather/New-York/Places/buffalo-snowfall-totals-snow-accumulation-averages.php)
Official eQUEST website and software documentation (https://www.doe2.com/equest/)
eQUEST 3.65 uses DOE-2.2 as its simulation engine; confirmed in multiple sources including NREL documentation and official website (https://energy-models.com/software/equest)
Default ground reflectance in DOE-2 and eQUEST is confirmed as 0.2 for unspecified ground surfaces (https://energy-models.com/forum/groundwater-reflectance)
File system inspection; file contains valid eQUEST/DOE-2 input format with SITE-PARAMETERS section

pi_items.verified_value: Current file does not contain GROUND-REFLECTANCE parameter in SITE-PARAMETERS section; would use default of 0.2
pi_items.source: Direct file inspection using grep and text reading; 3,258 line file confirmed to contain no GROUND-REFLECTANCE entries

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a real 4-storey building energy model (4StoreyBuilding.inp, 94.8 KB, available in the data directory) using eQUEST 3.65 with DOE-2.2 simulation engine. The building is located in Buffalo, NY, a region that receives approximately 92 inches of annual snowfall with persistent ground snow cover from December through early March. The task requires configuring monthly ground reflectance (albedo) values: 0.60 for winter months (Jan, Feb, Dec) representing older/compressed snow, 0.40 for March representing melting snow conditions, and 0.20 for summer months (Apr-Nov) representing bare ground. These values are scientifically reasonable approximations of actual snow and ground reflectance properties documented in albedo literature. The current project file does not specify ground reflectance values and would use the eQUEST/DOE-2 default of 0.20.
